name: Apple Jelly/bh&g
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash 3 pounds tart apples, removing blossom ends and stems. Do not peel or core.
2. Cut apples into small chunks.
3. In an 8- to 10-quart kettle or Dutch oven, combine apples with enough water to cover (about 5 cups).
4. Bring mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce heat and simmer until apples are soft, about 20 to 25 minutes.
5. Strain the apples and liquid through a jelly bag; measure 4 cups juice.
6. In the kettle, mix apple juice with 3 cups sugar until dissolved.
7. Bring mixture to a full rolling boil. Boil hard, uncovered, until syrup sheets a metal spoon (10 to 12 minutes).
8. Remove from heat and quickly skim off foam.
9. Ladle into hot sterilized jars, leaving 1/2 inch of headspace.
10. Wipe rims; seal with metal lids and screw bands.
11. Invert jars; turn upright and cool.
